children.heading: .—1So in original. No subsec. (b) has been enacted.1In General
children.label: a .—1So in original. No subsec. (b) has been enacted.1In General

children.text: provides a summary of railroad safety and hazardous materials compliance inspections and audits that Federal or State inspectors conducted in the prior fiscal year organized by type of alleged violation, including track, motive power and equipment, signal, grade crossing, operating practices, accident and incident reporting, and hazardous materials;

children.label: A
children.text: the number of civil penalties assessed;

children.label: B
children.text: the initial amount of civil penalties assessed;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of civil penalty cases settled;

children.label: D
children.text: the final amount of civil penalties assessed;

children.label: E
children.text: the difference between the initial and final amounts of civil penalties assessed;

children.label: F
children.text: the number of administrative hearings requested and completed related to hazardous materials transportation law violations or enforcement actions against individuals;

children.label: G
children.text: the number of cases referred to the Attorney General for civil or criminal prosecution; and

children.label: H
children.text: the number and subject matter of all compliance orders, emergency orders, or precursor agreements;
analyzes the effect of the number of inspections conducted and enforcement actions taken on the number and rate of reported accidents and incidents and railroad safety;

children.label: A
children.text: for each Class I railroad individually; and

children.label: i
children.text: Class II railroads;

children.label: ii
children.text: Class III railroads;

children.label: iii
children.text: hazardous materials shippers; and

children.label: iv
children.text: individuals;

children.label: A
children.text: the Locomotive Engineer Review Board;

children.label: B
children.text: an administrative hearing officer or administrative law judge; or

children.label: C
children.text: the Administrator of the Federal Railroad Administration;
provides an explanation regarding any changes in the Secretary’s or the Federal Railroad Administration’s enforcement programs or policies that may substantially affect the information reported; and
includes any additional information that the Secretary determines is useful to improve the transparency of its enforcement program.
